source_book: The 4 Disciplines of Execution (2nd ed., 2021) — Chris McChesney, Sean Covey, Jim Huling, Scott Thele, Beverly Walker
_comment: Member-voice debrief BEFORE walking into a team WIG Session, processing last week's kept/partial/missed commitment. Lures cover leader-misfire (session-lead receives accounts), personal-misfire (solo lapse), and chronic-misfire (3+ misses = redesign problem). Edge case = kept commitment but no understanding why (fire because Crisafulli discipline applies to wins too).

test_cases.prompt: I missed my commitment last week. The team WIG Session is tomorrow morning — what do I say? I don't want to make excuses but I also don't want to grovel.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d4-member-account-debrief; run the 8-step pre-session debrief — restate verbatim → classify (kept / partial / missed) → factual cause → learning → re-commit vs redesign → produce the ~30-second Account-segment script. Reject excuse-mode AND shame-mode.
test_cases.notes: positive scenario: classic missed-commitment pre-session prep (member voice, EN)

test_cases.prompt: 先週のコミットメントが果たせませんでした。明日 session があるんですが、どう振り返って何て言えばいいか整理したいです。
test_cases.expected_behavior: 4dx-d4-member-account-debrief を発動。8 ステップで verbatim 再述 → 分類 → 事実的原因 → 学び → 再 commit か再設計か → 30 秒の Account script を作る。言い訳・自虐・物語化を排除。
test_cases.notes: positive scenario: JP member-voice, missed-commitment, session imminent

test_cases.prompt: 上週的 commitment 沒達成，這次 session 我不知道怎麼面對。help me think through how to debrief 這件事。
test_cases.expected_behavior: 啟動 4dx-d4-member-account-debrief；走 8 步：逐字重述上週承諾 → kept/partial/missed 分類 → 事實原因（不要找藉口，也不要自責）→ 學到什麼 → 同 scope 再 commit / 縮小 / 重新設計 → 產出 30 秒的 Account 腳本。
test_cases.notes: positive scenario: zh-TW member-voice, missed-commitment, dread before session

test_cases.prompt: One of my team members missed their commitment last week. I'm running the WIG Session tomorrow — how should I address it without crushing them?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate this skill. The user is the LEADER asking how to receive a missed-commitment account (Susan-side dialogue, Ch. 10), not the member preparing what to say. Route to the team-wig-session-lead skill.
test_cases.notes: lure: leader-misfire — same 'missed commitment' keyword but leader-side facilitation, not member-side self-account

test_cases.prompt: I dropped my solo weekly review cadence three weeks ago and I'm trying to get back on track. No team, just me — help me restart.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate this skill. The user is in solo / no-team context with collapsed personal cadence. Route to 4dx-sustain-personal-momentum-rescue.
test_cases.notes: lure: personal-misfire — 'missed' / 'commitment'-adjacent vocabulary but solo lapse, not team-session pre-process

test_cases.prompt: I've missed my commitment four weeks in a row at this point. The session is tomorrow. What do I say?
test_cases.expected_behavior: The skill MAY fire on the trigger surface (session-tomorrow + missed-commitment is exactly the language signal), but per the Boundary section + step 6 decision rule, 3+ misses in a row is a COMMITMENT-DESIGN problem, not a debrief problem. Correct behavior: briefly acknowledge the trigger, then hand off to 4dx-d4-member-commitment-prep for redesign of the commitment shape (smaller scope, different mechanism). Do NOT run a fourth one-week debrief on the same broken commitment.
test_cases.notes: lure: chronic-misfire — surface looks like a debrief but the in-skill boundary correctly routes to redesign instead. The skill must recognize and route, not silently produce a stale debrief.

test_cases.prompt: Actually I kept my commitment last week — finished the deliverable, on time. But the team's lead measure didn't move much and I'm not sure if it was because of what I did or something else. WIG Session is Friday. Should I bother debriefing?
test_cases.expected_behavior: DO activate 4dx-d4-member-account-debrief. The skill explicitly covers kept commitments (Case 3 — Crisafulli's discipline of refusing favourable results without understanding the mechanism). Run the protocol with the kept-commitment branch in step 5 ('what did you learn about the lead-measure mechanism — did doing this actually move the lead?'). Produce a 30-second script that names: kept + the open mechanism question + the learning + the next-step (re-commit or sharpen the lead-measure understanding).
test_cases.notes: boundary: kept-but-mechanism-unclear is a real and underappreciated debrief case. The book's Crisafulli case anchors it. Skill should fire, not abstain.

ship_threshold_note: 100% to ship cleanly. >=80% allowed only with explicit failure analysis. <80% must redo Stage 2 A2/E/B. should_not_trigger has zero tolerance.
